verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Surfline and Surf-Forecast agree that the useful windows are poor.

conversationalSummary.text: Tomorrow is probably one to skip. Rey Cortiço at Lunch — 13:00 looks like the least-bad option, but not really something worth chasing. The wind looks like the main issue and could easily make it messy. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam first, but unless it looks surprisingly clean, I'd save the drive.

best.windFit: NW 15 km/h · onshore · poor wind fit
best.bestWind: ideal E/ENE/ESE, usable NE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 11 kJ, 8 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at low tide can still work on Belgas if the sandbars are lined up, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for lunch — 13:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
